summary: Matched the common fighter down-tilt state `ftCo_AttackLw3` by moving the 0x8008BB44-0x8008BFA4 assembly range into a new C file and wiring it into the build. The PR also renamed several adjacent input-check functions from address-style names to semantic `ftCo_*_CheckInput` names, added shared motion-var/common-data fields needed by the match, introduced a `GUARD(cond)` macro to express early-return IASA chains, and propagated the new names through common attack, wait, Game & Watch, Link, Fox, Samus, and header code. There were no PR body comments or review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_AttackLw3.h` declaring the new down-tilt common action callbacks and check function.

Removed most of the corresponding assembly from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`; the excerpt shows deletion of `ft_8008BB44` through the common AttackLw3 callbacks and retention/renaming of later smash attack input routines.
Added the new C object to `obj_files.mk` before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o`, preserving the mixed C/asm object order.

Renamed address-based prototypes/calls for smash and movement input checks, including `ft_8008BFC4` to `ftCo_AttackS4_CheckInput`, `ft_8008C830` to `ftCo_AttackHi4_CheckInput`, `ft_8008CB44` to `ftCo_AttackLw4_CheckInput`, `ft_800CAED0` to `ftCo_Jump_CheckInput`, `ft_800CA094` to `ftCo_Dash_CheckInput`, `ft_800D5F58` to `ftCo_Squat_CheckInput`, `ft_800C97DC` to `ftCo_Turn_CheckInput`, and `ft_800C9468` to `ftCo_Walk_CheckInput`.
Rewrote several IASA callback chains to use the new `GUARD(cond)` macro for immediate early returns when an input check succeeds.
Special-cased Game & Watch in the common down-tilt path: `decideFighter` calls `ftGw_AttackLw3_Enter` for `FTKIND_GAMEWATCH`, otherwise the common `doEnter` path is used.
Added `ftCommon_MotionVars.attacklw3.x0` at `fp+2340` and expanded `ftCommonData` fields around offsets `+B0` through `+E8`, including `xB0` used as the down-stick threshold.
Renamed Samus data attributes from `ftSamusAttributes` to `ftSs_DatAttrs` across multiple Samus special files and `ftSamus/types.h`, likely as cleanup required by touched headers/types.
smart_moves: Kept the new common action in its own `ftCo_AttackLw3.c`/`.h` pair instead of continuing to grow the catch-all `ft_08A4.s`, making future common attack work easier to search and isolate.
Used semantic names for nearby still-asm input functions, so newly decompiled C could read like behavior (`ftCo_AttackLw4_CheckInput`, `ftCo_Jump_CheckInput`) rather than address plumbing.
Introduced `GUARD(cond)` to preserve the common decomp pattern of ordered input checks with early exits while reducing deeply nested or long negated boolean expressions.
Modeled the Game & Watch exception in a small dispatcher (`decideFighter`) rather than duplicating the down-tilt entry logic everywhere.
Represented the `fp+2340` motion variable as a named union member `mv.co.attacklw3.x0`, improving type locality for a state-specific variable while preserving the offset evidence.
Used `MUST_MATCH` stack-padding blocks for functions with noted unused stack requirements, showing a matching tactic without forcing fake live logic into normal builds.
Kept residual unmatched code in asm and renamed labels/functions there where possible, allowing incremental decomp without requiring the whole `ft_08A4` translation unit to match at once.
decomp_lessons: For common fighter IASA code, preserving input-check order is essential; this PR’s attack IASA chains keep smash attacks, tilts, jab, jump/dash/squat/turn/walk in explicit ordered guard sequences.

When matching fighter state entry functions, state-specific `cmd_vars`, `mv.co.*` fields, callbacks such as `fp->cb.x21EC_callback`, and `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` flags often need to be named together to make the code line up.
Character-specific common-action overrides can be handled by switching on `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)->kind`; here Game & Watch down tilt enters `ftGw_AttackLw3_Enter` instead of the generic state.

Down-tilt input detection used both raw A-button state (`fp->input.x668 & HSD_Pad_A`) and vertical stick thresholds (`fp->input.lstick.y <= p_ftCommonData->xB0` plus `ftCommon_8007D964(fp) < -p_ftCommonData->x20`).
Light item throw down (`ftCo_MS_LightThrowLw`) is part of the same input path when `fp->item_gobj != NULL` and `ft_80094E54(fp)` succeeds, so attack matching must account for held-item branches.
Adding common-data field names for offsets consumed by a matched function can be preferable to leaving a filler array, because it reduces casts/magic offsets in the new C.
Build ordering matters for partial decomp: add the new C object before the remaining assembly object when carving a function range out of an asm file.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action files follow `ftCo_<Action>.c` and headers follow `ftCo_<Action>.h`, e.g. `ftCo_AttackLw3.c` and `ftCo_AttackLw3.h`.
Input test helpers are named `ftCo_<Action>_CheckInput`, e.g. `ftCo_AttackLw3_CheckInput`, `ftCo_AttackS4_CheckInput`, `ftCo_Jump_CheckInput`, and `ftCo_Walk_CheckInput`.
Common action callbacks use suffixes `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`.
Fighter kind enum evidence uses `FTKIND_GAMEWATCH` for character-specific dispatch.
Common motion-state enum evidence includes `ftCo_MS_AttackLw3` and `ftCo_MS_LightThrowLw`.
Samus data attributes were standardized from `ftSamusAttributes` to `ftSs_DatAttrs` in touched files.
Unknown but state-local command variable enum used `cmd_var_idx` with `cmd_unk0_bool`; unknown private helpers stayed conservative (`callUnk`, `doEnter`) rather than inventing precise behavior.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Carved matched functions out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` while leaving later functions in assembly, a standard partial-translation tactic.
Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` local byte arrays to reproduce unused stack allocation in selected functions without affecting normal non-matching builds.
Introduced a small fake wrapper marked `/// @todo This is fake.` around `decideFighter(gobj)` to satisfy codegen/control-flow needs in `ftCo_AttackLw3_Anim`.
Preserved exact early-return behavior with `GUARD(cond)` instead of converting the logic into large combined boolean expressions that are harder to match.
Kept comments with original addresses in the new header declarations (`/* 08BB44 */`, `/* 08BCFC */`, etc.) for traceability from asm to C.
Replaced hardcoded float constants from assembly with `NULL`, `0`, and `1` arguments in `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, relying on platform/types to emit the matching call shape.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available; conclusions are based on title, changed files, and diff evidence only.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove the fake `wrapper` or rename `callUnk`/`cmd_unk0_bool` in `ftCo_AttackLw3.c`.
Search for subsequent decomp of remaining `ft_08A4.s` functions after `ftCo_AttackLw3` was carved out.
Look for PRs that refine `ftCommonData` field names around offsets `xB0` through `xE8`.
Check whether later reviews standardized use of the global `GUARD(cond)` macro in IASA callbacks.
Search for follow-up renames of Samus `ftSs_DatAttrs` fields and cleanup of related special-move files.
